description: Company claims 'AI-powered' customer service chatbot, but uses rule-based Eliza-style responses with no ML components.
Public sector procurement labeled as 'AI-driven analytics' uses Excel macros and manual data entry.
Startup raises $10M claiming 'AI breakthrough' but uses thresholding and regex for document processing.
Healthcare provider markets 'AI diagnostics' that are simple statistical models from 1990s literature.
Educational platform claims 'AI grading' but uses predefined answer templates and keyword matching.
